news.title: The 2026 World Cup was full of spectacle, scandal and compromise
news.lede: This was a good rather than great World Cup on the field. In the background, things were a lot less edifying. Perhaps it’s too early, with the shreds of gold tape still fluttering through the New Jersey sky, to make a proper assessment. That will only come when everything has sunk in properly, when memories have had the chance to settle and shape themselves, when the consequences of certain events have become clear.

news.title: Trump show offers fitting fever-dream end to a World Cup of excess and damage
news.lede: Football appeared a sideshow for much of the last afternoon of a tournament nurtured to indulge the cult of the individual. It ended almost as Gianni Infantino and Donald Trump had planned. Earlier on a seemingly endless World Cup final day, the trophy had, in a scene perfectly of a piece with the event’s excess, been let out of a Louis Vuitton trunk. Now they shuffled up to the podium together and presented it to a beaming Rodri, fumbling the moment slightly when Trump lingered for Spain’s team photo.

news.title: 2030 FIFA World Cup will span across 3 continents and 6 countries, may expand to 64 teams
news.lede: Spain's midfielder Rodri lifts the trophy with his teammates after Spain won the 2026 World Cup football tournament final match between Spain and Argentina at the New York/New Jersey Stadium in East Rutherford on July 19, 2026. After 39 days, 72 group stage matches, the Round of 32, the Round of 16, the Quarterfinals, Semifinals, and third-place Bronze match, the 2026 FIFA World Cup concluded Sunday with Spain defeating Argentina 1 -0. Now the world will have to wait four more years for the next World Cup in 2030 which will span across three continents and six countries. Just like the 2026 World Cup, there will be three host countries for the 2030 FIFA World Cup.

news.title: Watch Spain World Cup parade live in Madrid
news.lede: Spain claimed its second World Cup title on Sunday, July 19, defeating Lionel Messi and Argentina 1-0 after extra time at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey. La Roja have returned home, where a massive celebration is set in the capital of Madrid on Monday, July 20. The team will travel in an open-top bus through central Madrid to Cibeles Square, where a ceremony will be held. The players will be greeted by as many as 1 million fans, as well as Spanish royalty and Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez.

news.title: Rodri's stock at all-time high as he adds to trophy collection
news.lede: Rodri became the first Spanish winner of the Golden Ball since the award was introduced in 1978. As Spain captain Rodri lifted the World Cup trophy high into the New Jersey sky, he added another trophy to his already glittering record. A Ballon d'Or, one European title, one Nations League, four Premier League trophies, one Champions League and numerous other domestic triumphs. And now the greatest prize of them all.

news.title: ‘A unique collective experience’: Spain media hail team’s World Cup triumph
news.lede: El Mundo marks country’s ‘unique collective experience’. El País highlights the side’s ‘conviction of hard work’. Sometimes football is about more than just football. As Spain’s still-breathless media pointed out on Monday, the national side’s victory against Argentina the previous night was an epochal event that had transcended talk of tactics, grumbles over hydration breaks and displays of distinctly ungentlemanly conduct.

news.title: Gangs of New Jersey: how the World Cup final descended into ugly scenes between Argentina and Spain
news.lede: A blow-by-blow account of how players from both sides fought in the aftermath of Sunday’s showpiece. The World Cup final took an ugly turn at the end of extra time as Spain were crowned champions. Here’s what happened, frame by frame. As the referee, Slavko Vincic, blows the full-time whistle, Spain’s substitutes run onto the pitch to celebrate World Cup glory with their teammates.

news.title: FIFA opens disciplinary investigation into Argentina after chaotic World Cup final defeat
news.lede: FIFA has opened disciplinary proceedings following the heated scenes that overshadowed Spain’s World Cup final victory over Argentina, with several members of the South American side set to come under scrutiny. Spain claimed their second World Cup title with a 1-0 extra-time win in New Jersey, but the post-match celebrations were marred by confrontations involving players and coaching staff from Argentina. According to reports, FIFA’s disciplinary committee will review the referee’s reports, official match documentation and video footage before deciding whether any sanctions are warranted. Midfielder Leandro Paredes is expected to be a central figure in the investigation after receiving a red card for violent conduct following the final whistle.
